annotations.label: Characteristic class (informal)

anchor.snippet: a characteristic class is a way of associating to each principal bundle

annotations.note: Mathlib has vector/fiber bundle infrastructure but no notion of principal bundle nor of characteristic classes.

annotations.label: Isomorphism classes functor of principal G-bundles
annotations.note: No formalization of principal G-bundles or their classifying functor in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characteristic class as natural transformation

anchor.snippet: A characteristic class c of principal G -bundles is then a natural transformation

annotations.note: Mathlib has natural transformations but no principal-bundle functor on which to define characteristic classes.

annotations.label: Naturality of characteristic classes

anchor.snippet: a characteristic class associates to each principal

annotations.note: No characteristic class theory exists in Mathlib, so naturality is not stated.

annotations.label: Characteristic numbers

anchor.snippet: one can obtain integers from characteristic classes, called characteristic numbers

annotations.note: Characteristic numbers are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pairing with the fundamental class

anchor.snippet: one can pair a product of characteristic classes of total degree n with the fundamental class

annotations.note: No fundamental class of a manifold and no characteristic-class pairing are formalized.

annotations.label: Characteristic number formula
annotations.note: Mathlib lacks the prerequisites (characteristic classes, fundamental class) to state this formula.

annotations.label: Characteristic numbers via de Rham cohomology

anchor.snippet: one can take differential forms representing the characteristic classes

annotations.note: Mathlib has no de Rham cohomology of manifolds nor Chern-Weil theory.

annotations.label: Characteristic numbers for non-orientable manifolds

anchor.snippet: This also works for non-orientable manifolds

annotations.note: No mod-2 characteristic-number theory for non-orientable manifolds in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characteristic numbers determine cobordism class

anchor.snippet: two manifolds are (respectively oriented or unoriented) cobordant if and only if their characteristic numbers are equal

annotations.note: Cobordism is not formalized in Mathlib, so Thom's theorem is unavailable.

annotations.label: Stable characteristic class

anchor.snippet: a stable class is one that does not change when one adds a trivial bundle

annotations.note: Stability of characteristic classes is not defined since the underlying classes are absent.

annotations.label: Stability of Chern, Stiefel–Whitney, Pontryagin; Euler unstable

anchor.snippet: the Chern class , Stiefel–Whitney class , and Pontryagin class are stable , while the Euler class is unstable

annotations.note: Chern, Stiefel–Whitney, Pontryagin, and Euler classes are not defined in Mathlib (the 'Pontryagin'/'Chern'/'characteristic' hits refer to Pontryagin duality and characteristic functions in probability).

annotations.label: Euler class is unstable

anchor.snippet: This is not the case for the Euler class

annotations.note: The Euler class of a vector bundle is not formalized in Mathlib.
